title: Man found with zip ties and duct tape charged with assaulting 5 women at UCLA
summary: A man was charged Wednesday with assaulting five women in less than an hour at UCLA last week, officials said
story: A man was charged Wednesday with assaulting five women in less than an hour at UCLA last week, officials said.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

Olumuyiwa Akindahunsi, 29, is accused of sexually assaulting two women and trying to sexually assault a third at UCLA’s Westwood campus, according to the Los Angeles County District Attorney’s Office. Two other women were physically assaulted.
The assaults occurred between 11:30 p.m. Thursday and shortly after midnight, the prosecutor’s office said.
Akindahunsi was found with zip ties, duct tape and a paracord when authorities took him into custody, the school’s police department said in a statement last week.
Four of the women are students, the prosecutor’s office said Wednesday.
The school police department described Akindahunsi as homeless. The district attorney’s office said he isn’t a student and didn’t have permission to be on campus.

“Our university campuses need to be safe and protected spaces for our students, faculty, staff and community — and we will do everything in our power to ensure that this happens,” District Attorney Nathan Hochman said in the release.

Akindahunsi was charged with one count of attempted kidnapping to commit a forcible rape, three counts of assault with the attempt to commit a forcible rape during the commission of a first-degree burglary, and two felony counts of assault likely to produce great bodily injury, according to the release.
Court records didn’t list an attorney for Akindahunsi. Jail records show he’s being held without bail.

The first alleged assault was reported on the main pedestrian walkway on campus. A student said a man approached her from behind and used force to steal her cellphone before he fled, campus police said.

Minutes later, the district attorney’s office said, a man allegedly followed two women into their dorm and sexually assaulted one of them. The other woman was physically assaulted, the prosecutor’s office said.

Akindahunsi then entered another dorm building and allegedly sought to kidnap a woman in a hallway to sexually assault her, the district attorney’s office alleged. The woman broke free, and the man fled.
Akindahunsi is accused of sexually assaulting a fifth woman in the hallway of her dorm before a nearby resident intervened and he fled, the district attorney’s office said.
University police officers found Akindahunsi near a campus parking structure, the department said. Police said multiple accusers and witnesses identified him as the suspect.

title: SpaceX aims to make its stock market debut at $1.77 trillion value
summary: Elon Musk’s space company said in a securities filing that it hopes to sell over 555 million shares at its debut for $135 apiece.
story: Elon Musk’s SpaceX announced Wednesday that it intends to make its stock market debut next week at a $1.77 trillion valuation.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

The highly anticipated initial public offering, which is expected to be the largest ever, would raise $75 billion in fresh cash for the company. SpaceX said in a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission that it hopes to sell more than 555 million shares at $135 apiece.
Musk is the CEO, chief technical officer and chairman of the company. After the IPO, he “will hold approximately 82.4% of the voting power of our common stock,” SpaceX said in the filing.
If the $135 share price holds up through the company’s debut on the Nasdaq, most likely in about a week, it could make Musk the world’s first trillionaire.
The final price at which SpaceX’s shares will begin trading has not yet been set, however.

SpaceX plans to use the money raised through the offering to invest in its many businesses. In recent months, SpaceX expanded into a number of non-space industries when it acquired Musk’s xAI artificial intelligence company, which also owns the social media network X.

SpaceX will also instantaneously become one of the largest public companies in the world when it begins trading. At the $1.77 trillion value, it would rank as the seventh-largest publicly traded company in the world.
SpaceX will also be Musk’s second trillion-dollar public company. His first, Tesla, was worth $1.6 trillion at the close of trading Wednesday.
The particular way Musk and SpaceX are beginning to shop around the IPO is very unusual.
Typically, a company proposes a price range at which it wants to sell its shares. Then, the evening before trading commences, it nails down a final price around that range.
But in Wednesday’s filing, SpaceX offered only the fixed $135 price.
Given Musk’s global name recognition, the offering may require very little marketing to prospective investors, unlike many other IPOs.
SpaceX says it anticipates offering individual retail investors the chance to buy shares when it debuts through trading platforms, including Schwab, Fidelity, Robinhood, SoFi and E*Trade.

title: House votes to rebuke Trump over war with Iran
summary: The House offered a rare rebuke to President Donald Trump, passing a Democratic-led measure to end his war with Iran over objections from Republican leadership.
story: WASHINGTON — The House offered a rare rebuke to President Donald Trump on Wednesday, passing a Democratic-led measure to end his war with Iran over objections from Republican leadership.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
It was one of two Democratic-led measures opposed by the White House that advanced in the GOP-led House. Lawmakers later passed a motion that would unlock a vote on sending aid to Ukraine.

The Iran war powers resolution, offered by Rep. Gregory Meeks of New York, the top Democrat on the Foreign Affairs Committee, had been heading for a vote before the House left for its Memorial Day recess May 21. But it was abruptly pulled from the floor when it appeared too many Republicans were absent to defeat it.

On Wednesday, it passed 215-208, with four Republicans joining all Democrats in voting yes: Brian Fitzpatrick of Pennsylvania, Thomas Massie of Kentucky, Tom Barrett of Michigan and Warren Davidson of Ohio.

The resolution directs Trump to remove U.S. armed forces from hostilities with Iran, unless Congress votes to declare war or authorizes using military force against it. It would not force him to end the conflict, however; it is a symbolic expression of disapproval of the war with Iran.
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google

Republicans had rejected three other attempts to pass a war powers resolution this year. The most recent vote ended in a 212-212 tie, and Democrats celebrated, saying that more Americans are turning against the war and that it was only a matter of time before they would be successful.

Wednesday’s vote gives momentum for the resolution in the Senate, which had already advanced its own war powers resolution on the floor last month but had not yet held a final vote. The Senate version has teeth, however, and it would require Trump to end the war without congressional approval. But it would need to pass the House, and then Trump could veto it.
A White House official responded to the House vote by noting that a handful of GOP lawmakers were absent for it and that previous efforts on similar resolutions were unsuccessful.

The House resolution was opposed by Speaker Mike Johnson, R-La., and most Republicans, who complained that it undermines Trump and his top negotiators when they are trying to secure a nuclear deal with Iran. Foreign Affairs Committee Chairman Brian Mast, R-Fla., called Wednesday’s action “just a total BS vote.”

“I think there’s no Democrat, no Republican, that can tell you what forces they would want pulled from Iran. There’s really nothing they actually want pulled from there. They just want a stupid political vote, which is what this is,” Mast said Wednesday. That “weakens the president’s hands as he’s negotiating with Iran.”

In the same vote series Wednesday, the House voted 218-204 in favor of a motion to “discharge,” or bring forward, legislation that would provide aid to Ukraine. That vote took place after the so-called discharge petition reached 218 signatures — a simple majority of the chamber — on May 13, allowing supporters to bypass Johnson and GOP leadership and bring the measure directly to the floor.
Rep. Kevin Kiley of California, a Republican-turned-independent, joined all Democrats and two Republicans, Fitzpatrick and Don Bacon of Nebraska, in signing the Ukraine petition.
Kiley and six Republicans voted Wednesday to discharge the bill. That sets up a final vote Thursday.

“This vote is not a process vote,” said Meeks, who also led the discharge effort. “It’s a statement on whether this Congress and all of its members stand with and support Ukraine and the people of Ukraine and its fight for freedom, its fight for democracy and its fight for liberty.”

title: Decision to allow three boys convicted of rape to walk free sparks fury and debate in U.K.
summary: LONDON — A judge’s decision to spare three teenage boys found guilty of rape at knifepoint and other serious sexual offenses from a custodial sentence has sparked outrage across the U.K.
story: LONDON — A judge’s decision to spare three teenage boys found guilty of rape at knifepoint and other serious sexual offenses from a custodial sentence has sparked outrage across the U.K.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

Judge Nicholas Rowland’s decision to issue youth rehabilitation orders, or child community sentences, to the trio was widely criticized in Britain’s press. Prime Minister Keir Starmer called the outcome “distressing.”
Several campaigns calling for the judge’s removal have also been launched on social media, including one petition that was signed by more than 200,000 people.
The case stems from two separate attacks perpetrated two months apart by the convicted teens, who were 13 and 14 at the time. The victims were two girls, ages 15 and 14.
Member of Parliament Jess Phillips, a longtime advocate for women and girls’ rights, told NBC News in a video interview last week that she was “horrified” by the lenient sentences.

“Immediately I just thought that this is the wrong sentence,” said Phillips, who served as the U.K.’s minister for safeguarding and violence against women and girls between July 2024 and May 2026. “No doubt about that.”

Along with other victim advocates, Phillips warned of the chilling effect the case could have for future sexual assault survivors when weighing whether to come forward. The ruling has also revived questions about whether the British justice system can be trusted to advocate for victims of sexual violence and has spurred calls for reform.

Starmer announced that the case, which he described as “distressing as a politician” and “as a father,” would go to the Court of Appeal; Attorney General Richard Hermer said he received multiple requests to review the sentences.

“There is an epidemic of violence against women and girls in this country,” Hermer said in a statement, “and this government will not hesitate in taking action to ensure all women and girls feel safe and have confidence in the justice system.”
Details of the attacks in Hampshire, a county southwest of London, came to light last week. (Underage perpetrators are not named in the U.K.)
“We have a word here in Britain called ‘gobsmacked,’” Ann Olivarius, an American-British attorney and advocate for women’s rights, told NBC News in a phone interview Friday.
“I just felt like there was a brick that landed in the face of all women, of social justice, of everything we believe in,” added Olivarius. “It violates the rule of law.”
Her comments came after one of the victims, speaking anonymously to Britain’s public broadcaster, the BBC, said that hearing the sentencing outcome felt like a “rock straight in my face.”
“What was the point of putting me through that?” she said of the trial.

During the trial, Southampton Crown Court heard how one of the victims, aged 15 at the time, was raped by two of the boys in an underpass after arranging to meet one of them for a date, according to a news release from the Crown Prosecution Service.
Video of the rape, which lasted 90 minutes, was shared on social media, prosecutors told the court, according to Britain’s Press Association (PA) news agency.
Months later, in January 2025, a girl, aged 14 at the time, was assaulted after becoming separated from her friends, the CPS said.

She was threatened with a knife and forced to leave her mobile phone and AirTag in a shop so she could not be tracked. She was then taken to a secluded area and raped by two of the boys while others “encouraged the offending and filmed the assaults,” it said.
The perpetrators left the scene “when they believed they had been disturbed,” the CPS said, adding that the victim was later found distressed and reported the incident to police.
All three boys accused in the case were convicted on multiple counts of rape on March 5.

One of the boys, identified only as “Boy A” by the CPS, was convicted of two counts of rape and one count of taking indecent images of a child. A second, “Boy B,” was convicted on six counts of rape. Both were sentenced to three-year youth rehabilitation  orders.
The third boy was convicted of two counts of rape and has been sentenced to an 18-month youth rehabilitation order.
As a result they will avoid custody, remain at home and complete a plan tailored and overseen by their local Youth Justice Service.
At their sentencing, Judge Rowland said he wanted to “avoid criminalizing these children unnecessarily,” the Press Association reported.

The court heard that one of the boys had an IQ in the “bottom 1% of his contemporaries” and was diagnosed with ADHD, according to the outlet. Another was also diagnosed with ADHD, while the third was described as having a “mild cognitive impairment,” the agency reported.

Both Phillips and Olivarius agreed the sentences sent a detrimental message to both victims and perpetrators that even with a conviction, rape and other forms of sexual assault will be met with leniency.

“Just don’t do it again in the next year or two and you’ll be fine ... that’s the message that there is now. It’s a totally free crime,” Olivarius said, adding that she believed the judge “should be removed from the bench” over the sentencing.

Phillips said that while she was in favor of some form of rehabilitation for perpetrators of sexual assault, she believed that the assailants in this case should have also been “incarcerated for their crimes as a matter of both punishment and public safety.”
She added that she did not believe any adequate rehabilitation program for young people currently exists in the U.K. to address the realities of sexual violence and misogyny in society.
The Sentencing Council for England and Wales maintains that even in serious cases, a “custodial sentence should always be a measure of last resort for children and young people.”

In the U.S., by contrast, every state has at least one transfer mechanism allowing young people to be charged in the adult criminal legal system, according to The Sentencing Project, which advocates for “effective and humane” responses to crime that minimize imprisonment.
In both the U.K. and the U.S., there have long been challenges in seeing reports of sexual violence result in charges and, ultimately, convictions.
In 2024, there were at least 71,227 incidents of rape recorded by police in England and Wales, according to Rape Crisis England & Wales, a charity.
By the end of 2024, the charity said, charges were brought in just 2.7% of those cases.
“In other words... fewer than 3 in 100 rapes recorded by police in 2024 resulted in someone being charged that same year,” it says on its website.

In the U.S., just last year, an NBC News investigation found that less than 4% of reported rapes, sexual assaults and child sex abuse allegations in certain cities ever result in a sex crime conviction.

Meanwhile, Phillips said, victims are left to live with the impacts of the perpetrators’ actions — with the survivors in this case forced to continue to relive their ordeals as the case continues on to the Court of Appeal.

Speaking with the BBC, the other victim in the case said she lived in fear that she was going to see her assailants again, even with restraining orders in place, as she spoke of the devastating impact of their crimes.
“I feel like no matter what I do, I can always feel their hands on me, no matter how much I’ve scrubbed,” she told the broadcaster.
“It’s always there and it just doesn’t feel like my body anymore.”

If you or someone you know has been sexually assaulted, call the National Sexual Assault Hotline at 1-800-656-4673. The hotline, run by the Rape, Abuse, & Incest National Network (RAINN), can put you in contact with your local rape crisis center. You can also access RAINN’s online chat service at rainn.org/get-help.

title: San Francisco 49ers’ Brandon Aiyuk is issued arrest warrant after posting speeding video
summary: San Francisco 49ers wide receiver Brandon Aiyuk was issued an arrest warrant in Northern California after he posted a video online that appears to show him driving at high speeds.
story: San Francisco 49ers wide receiver Brandon Aiyuk was issued an arrest warrant in Northern California after he posted a video online that appears to show him driving at high speeds.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

Aiyuk is accused of engaging in the misdemeanor of “exhibition of speed” on a highway “on or about” Dec. 20, according to a request for an arrest warrant filed in February. The date correlates with when a YouTube video was posted to Aiyuk’s account showing him driving a Cadillac CT5-V Blackwing.

He introduces the car in the video as the Cadillac’s answer to Dodge’s Hellcat model before he cuts to a clip of someone behind the wheel. Aiyuk’s face is not in the clip, but the driver is wearing red sweatpants, which Aiyuk was wearing earlier in the video.
The digital speedometer in the frame shows the vehicle driving as high as 111 mph at one point.
Aiyuk posted an apology this year, saying he would not post car content with speeding in the future.
“Was Praying with my son tonight and wouldn’t want anybody else to miss out on an opportunity to do the same with their loved ones!” he wrote. “My apologies.”
Representatives for Aiyuk and the 49ers did not immediately respond to a request for comment Wednesday.
It’s unclear whether the warrant is active. Court records show that a traffic case was filed against Aiyuk in Santa Clara County in January and that it remains open, but it lists no hearings.
The Santa Clara County District Attorney’s Office did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

Aiyuk is a native Californian whom the 49ers drafted from Arizona State in 2020 as the second of their two first-round picks. He signed a $120 million deal with the 49ers in 2024, just before he tore both his anterior cruciate and medial collateral ligaments.

The team voided the guaranteed money in his contract last year after Aiyuk failed to show up for rehab sessions, according to NBC Sports. John Lynch, the team’s general manager, said in January that the 49ers would move on from Aiyuk in the offseason, but he is still listed on the reserve squad.

title: Student who called out CBS at News Emmys says Scott Pelley did the ‘right thing’
summary: Moments before Santiago Campos took the stage at the News & Documentary Emmy Awards in New York City to accept a CBS News-funded scholarship in front of some of the most powerful people in TV news, veteran “60 Minutes” correspondent Scott Pelley took the time to offer the high school senior some advice.
story: Moments before Santiago Campos took the stage at the News & Documentary Emmy Awards in New York City to accept a CBS News-funded scholarship in front of some of the most powerful people in TV news, veteran “60 Minutes” correspondent Scott Pelley took the time to offer the high school senior some advice.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
“He told me not to leave the stage immediately after doing my remarks, to stand there,” Campos recalled. “Let it all soak in.”

Pelley appeared to follow his own advice as he introduced Campos to accept the Mike Wallace Memorial Scholarship, first recognizing fellow “60 Minutes” correspondent Sharyn Alfonsi from her seat in the audience before describing the teenager as “passionate, careful and hands-on.”

Then, after yielding the microphone to Campos, the legendary journalist stood close as Campos used his speech to criticize CBS News’ direction under Paramount Skydance owner David Ellison and editor-in-chief Bari Weiss, which he viewed as a threat to journalistic independence. In his speech, he said the “recent direction of the outlet stains the legacy of Mike Wallace,” referring to the scholarship’s legendary CBS News namesake, and spoke out against “corporate elites” taking over journalism.
After Campos’ 90-second speech, Pelley put his arm around him and shook his hand in front of the applauding crowd, before telling the teenager: “We need young people like you right behind us.”
“I know that Mike Wallace is looking down on you with pride at this very moment,” Pelley added.

The 18-year-old high school senior from the Washington, D.C., area told NBC News that at the time he wondered whether Pelley knew about his speech. Days later, after Pelley was fired amid escalating turmoil at CBS News, the exchange took on new significance.

Over the past week, Alfonsi confirmed that her CBS News deal had expired, and several other key senior staff members were let go. Tensions between “60 Minutes” staffers and management reached an all-time high during a Monday meeting to introduce executive producer Nick Bilton, where Pelley openly challenged leadership and accused Weiss of “murdering” the storied newsmagazine that debuted in 1968.
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google
The next day, Pelley was fired after a nearly 40-year run at CBS News.
Pelley’s departure has left “60 Minutes,” which just ended its 58th season, in limbo as staffers grapple with questions about the program’s future.

The show’s employees now feel “completely adrift” and are struggling with “great uncertainty” about what might happen to the three remaining full-time correspondents, Lesley Stahl, Bill Whitaker and Jon Wertheim, a source with direct knowledge of the matter who spoke on the condition of anonymity previously told NBC News. (Stahl, Whitaker and Wertheim have not publicly commented on Pelley’s ouster.)
On Wednesday, Weiss tried to temper fears and explain the decision to fire Pelley in a call with network employees, saying newsroom leaders could not “find a way back” with the veteran journalist.

“I hope I speak, I know I speak for myself, and I hope I speak for everyone here when I say that I’m only interested in working in a newsroom that is built on trust and mutual respect. We cannot do our work without it,” Weiss told CBS News employees Wednesday.

Pelley, however, rejected that characterization in a statement and insisted that at no point during his Tuesday meeting did anyone “suggest that there could be steps taken by either side that would lead to a resolution.”
CBS News spokespeople did not immediately respond to a request for comment on Pelley’s statement.

Noting that he felt compelled to make his own now-viral speech because he believed that his silence about CBS News “would be complicit about what’s going on,” Campos called Pelley’s decision to speak out the “right thing to do.”

“I think a lot of people can misinterpret the outcome as something, something negative and something to be afraid of,” Campos said. “I think he did what the situation demanded of him and faced the consequences. Even if the consequences were not just.”

The teenager, who is set to attend Georgetown University in the fall, added that Pelley’s comments should embolden CBS staffers to “fight for a greater cause.” When asked about his scholarship, Campos added that “the money is basically secured” and he does not believe “CBS has any way of pulling a scholarship.”

“Fear is understandable, but it’s not an acceptable excuse to be complicit in what’s going on,” Campos added. “They need to put their jobs on the line in order to fight for a change in a greater cause.”

title: After Auburn student goes missing in Japan, family fears he’s ‘emotionally distressed’
summary: Auburn University student James “Weston” Higginbotham went missing in Japan, with loved ones fearing he may be “emotionally distressed.”
story: An Auburn University engineering student went missing in Japan, his family and officials said Wednesday, and loved ones feared he could be “emotionally distressed.”
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
James “Weston” Higginbotham, 20, was last seen Friday in Kyoto, his mother, Nancy Higginbotham, said in a statement.
“Unfortunately, the authorities did not find Weston today,” she said in a statement posted late Wednesday.
The family is grateful for the efforts of Japanese police, FBI agents, U.S. diplomats, Alabama officials and “the wonderful Japanese volunteers,” Nancy Higginbotham said.
“I know the amount of resources they are pouring into finding our son is immense,” she added.
Higginbotham, a junior who studies biosystems engineering at the Alabama university, was traveling with his family when he vanished, Auburn said.
Higginbotham grew up in Hoover, Alabama, and graduated from Spain Park High School.
“Our hearts are with the Higginbotham family during this difficult time, and we remain hopeful for a positive resolution,” Hoover Mayor Nick Drezis said in a statement Wednesday.
Higginbotham was last spotted at Yamashina Station in Kyoto, according to his mother.
“We don’t know if he got back on the train at any point. Weston is a very experienced hiker and this town has beautiful nature trails,” her statement said.
“He may have intentionally chosen a quiet trail from Yamashina, especially Bishamon-do, Lake Biwa Canal, Misasagi, Keage/Nanzen-ji, Kyoto Trail, Mount Otowa, or Daigo/Kami-Daigo.”
She added: “He may be emotionally distressed, so this is urgent.”

A State Department spokesperson said in a statement Wednesday that the Trump administration is aware of the reports that Higginbotham was missing in Japan, and that the administration “has no higher priority than the safety of Americans.”
Higginbotham’s parents said they’re encouraged by the work of Japanese first responders and that social media is sharing the family’s story.
“We’re confident we’re going to find him,” his father, Keith Higginbotham, said in a video statement Tuesday.
“We’re going to find him because of everybody’s help and posting and getting the word out so we can find the person who’s going to find him.”

title: Navy vet charged with murder after attack on man known for pro-Trump displays
summary: A U.S.
story: A U.S. Navy veteran accused of fatally assaulting a Southern California man known for his pro-Trump house decorations was charged Wednesday with murder, the San Diego County district attorney said.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
Thomas Caleb Butler, 32, was charged last month with attempted murder after the attack but before the man’s death.
Kerry Sheron, 69, was assaulted outside his home in Escondido on May 20. Sheron, whose family has identified him as an Army veteran, was hospitalized in critical condition and died four days later.
A hearing for Butler has been scheduled for Friday.
He previously had been charged with attempted murder, elder abuse, criminal threats and battery after the assault.
Butler’s attorney and family declined to comment.

“The violent assault and murder of Kerry Sheron outside his own home has left his family and community shaken and grieving the   veteran’s senseless loss of life,” San Diego County District Attorney Summer Stephan said Wednesday.
“The investigation will continue to seek answers to the relevant aspects of this case.”
Sheron’s cause and manner of death have not been released.
An official with the San Diego County District Attorney’s Office previously described the assault as unprovoked.
Deputy District Attorney Ross Garcia accused Butler of striking Sheron once in the jaw and continuing to strike Sheron in the head after he fell to the ground, NBC San Diego reported.
Garcia did not identify a possible motive and said it’s unclear if the men knew each other.

Sheron’s stepdaughter, Alejandra Nava, described her father as a caring man who worked as a chef at local retirement homes. His home in Escondido, north of San Diego, was covered in American flags and signs celebrating Donald Trump.
His wife told NBC San Diego that they’d previously been threatened over the decorations.

Butler served in the Navy as an information service technician between 2011 and 2023. He was stationed in Virginia, Florida, California and Illinois, according to his service record, and he earned medals indicating that he was deployed in the war on terrorism and in support of counterterrorism operations.

title: The 51+ best early Amazon Prime Day 2026 deals you can shop now: Bissell, Le Creuset, Levi’s, and more (live updates)
summary: We’re tracking the best early Amazon Prime Day 2026 deals live, including discounts on tech, home, beauty, kitchen products and more before the four-day sale begins.
story: Amazon Prime Day 2026 officially starts on Jun. 23, but Prime members don’t have to wait to shop. Some of the best early Prime Day Deals are already live, including a handful that are currently at their lowest price ever, like the Bissell CleanView Upright Vacuum, Amazon Fire TV Soundbar Plus, Sony WH-CH720N Noise-Canceling Headphones and Levi’s 94 Baggy Jean Shorts.

I frequently cover shopping events like Prime Day, Black Friday and Cyber Monday as a reporter for NBC Select, so I know how to find deals that are actually worth buying. I spend weeks preparing for Amazon’s biggest sale of the year, which includes combing through thousands of discounts to find the best early Prime Day deals. Each one I recommend below is highly rated, as well as at its lowest price in at least three months, with discounts up to 63 percent off. I’ll frequently update this list prior to Prime Day.

Bissell CleanView Upright Vacuum

Bissell’s corded vacuum has powerful suction to pick up debris on all types of floors, and a large 2-liter container that holds multiple days of dirt. It comes with four attachments you can add to the end of the hose to access all the corners of your home, including hard-to-reach spaces like under furniture. You get a crevice tool, TurboBrush Tool for upholstery, dusting brush and extension wand with your purchase.
Waterpik Cordless Pulse 3100

Waterpik’s water flossers are some of our all-time favorite oral care products, and this cordless model earned the American Dental Association’s Seal of Acceptance. It has two pressure settings, comes with two tips and is water-resistant so you can use it in the shower. There’s a built-in reservoir on the tool’s handle that holds enough water for up to 45 seconds of use, and its battery gives you up to four weeks of use per charge, according to the brand.
Blissy Silk Sleep Mask

One of the most standout features of this silk eye mask that shoppers consistently mention is how soft it is. The luxe fabric has been praised for being gentle on the skin, and the mask is effective for blocking out light, according to reviewers. One even wrote that the sleep mask has helped their sister’s headaches. The elastic band on the back allows it to fit snugly yet comfortably. It’s an easy alternative to spending lots of money on blackout drapes when you want to keep sunlight out of your eyes while you rest.
Olay Regenerist Micro-Sculpting Cream

This daily moisturizer is formulated with triple collagen peptide, niacinamide and pro-vitamin B5, which are ingredients that target signs of aging — they help reduce the appearance of wrinkles, strengthen and hydrate the skin barrier, brighten skin tone, make the skin feel firmer and plumper, and smooth out texture over time, according to the brand. The cream is fragrance-free, making it suitable for sensitive-skin. It also absorbs quickly and has a non-oily feel, according to reviewers.
Le Creuset Nonstick Pro 8-inch Fry Pan

This 8-inch nonstick pan has a textured interior that helps you brown and sear food. It’s made from anodized aluminum, a material that helps it heat up quickly and distribute heat evenly, according to the brand. It’s compatible with all stovetops, oven-safe up to 500 degrees Fahrenheit, dishwasher-safe and works with metal utensils.
Anker Zolo Magnetic Wireless Charger (2 pack)

Instead of plugging these chargers into your device’s port, they magnetically attach to the back of compatible iPhones and AirPod cases, which prevents them from getting in the way while you’re using the device. They also have long 5-foot cables and come with cable ties so you can neatly wrap up the cords when you’re traveling with or storing them.
Paula's Choice Clear Pore Normalizing Facial Cleanser

Paula’s Choice formulated this cleanser for those with acne-prone skin. It’s made with salicylic acid, a chemical exfoliant that unclogs pores and reduces redness from breakouts, as well as pro-vitamin B5, which softens skin, according to the brand. The face wash helps remove dirt, makeup and excess oil, and Paula’s Choice recommends using it twice a day.
Ring Indoor Cam (2 pack)

Upgrade your home’s security system with these indoor cameras that let you livestream footage through the Ring app — you can evenly see clearly in the dark thanks to  Color Night Vision. You’re able to hear and speak to people (or pets) with the app and get real-time alerts, plus the cameras respond to Amazon Alexa voice commands. They have privacy covers and mics that shut-off when you want, and you can mount them or stand them up.
Wüsthof Gourmet 4-Piece Chef's Knife Set

Cooking experts we’ve talked to about kitchen knives say Wüsthof’s have fantastic craftsmanship and will last you forever. This set comes with three types: an 8-inch chef’s knife, a 2.75-inch paring knife and a 4.5-inch utility knife. You also get a knife sharpener. The lightweight knives are made with sharp stainless steel blades and have easy-grip molded handles with built-in hand guards for safer cutting and chopping.
Thermacell E-Series Rechargeable Mosquito Repeller

Reviewers say they’re impressed by how effective this mosquito repellent is, with one even calling it a “game-changer” for spending time outside. The cordless device continuously diffuses a repellent into the air, creating a 20-foot protection zone around you, according to the brand. It comes with a few refill repellent cartridges to get you started, as well as a charging base — a full battery gives you about 12 hours of use, says Thermacell.
Delsey Paris Cruise 3.0 Hardside Expandable Luggage

If you’re an over-packer, this hard-shell carry-on suitcase’s expandable interior is exactly what you need. It has two compartments for all your stuff, as well as elastic steps and a mesh divider to help keep you organized. It’s designed with spinner wheels, top and bottom carrying handles, a built-in TSA-approved lock and a dedicated sleeve that you can add your own power bank and charging cord to.
Blink Outdoor 2K+ Wireless Security 5 Camera System

Monitor what’s going on outside your home with Blink’s Outdoor Camera System, which comes with five cameras that are designed to withstand inclement weather. They run on AA batteries that last up to two years, according to the brand, and you can livestream footage, hear, speak to and zoom in on visitors using the Blink app. The cameras have color vision in low light, and each one comes with its own mounting kit.
Levi’s Women’s 94 Baggy Shorts

I bought these jean shorts during Prime Day 2025, and because I wear them so much, I’m buying a pair in every color this year. They’re loose enough that I’m always comfortable, but not so much that they look too casual, and I swear they get softer every time I wear them. They have a somewhat high waistband, which I prefer, and hit in the mid-thigh area. I wear a medium and find that they fit true to size.
Belkin BoostCharge 3-in-1 Magnetic Foldable Wireless Charger

Belkin’s Charging Station lets you repower your iPhone, Apple Watch and AirPods simultaneously. Each device magnetically attaches to its spot on the stand, and it folds flat when you’re not using it, making it travel-friendly.
Ultimate Ears Wonderboom 4 Bluetooth Speaker

Ultimate Ears’ Wonderboom 4 Bluetooth Speaker is water-resistant and floats, which NBC Select reporter Harry Rabinowitz can attest to, making the ideal choice for anyone who plans to use it outside for pool days, camping trips or rounds of golf. Rabinowitz has used the speaker for years and says despite its compact size, it can get very loud. There’s a built-in fabric loop on the top of the device, which you can connect a carabiner clip to. A full battery gives you about 14 hours of use, according to the brand.
Sony WH-CH720N Noise-Canceling Wireless Headphones

“These are a good pair of budget over-ear headphones that are super duper lightweight — it barely feels like any weight on your head when you’re wearing them, which is nice,” says Rabinowitz. They have a built-in microphone and Amazon Alexa voice assistant, and you can alternate between numerous listening modes, including noise-cancellation. The headphones give you up to 35 hours of listening on a full battery, according to the brand.

title: Marcello Hernández to Bring the Laughs as Host of the 2026 ESPYS
summary: Marcello Hernández is taking over the ESPYS. The “Saturday Night Live” star will host the annual sports awards show.
story: Marcello Hernández is taking over the ESPYS.
The “Saturday Night Live” star is bringing the laughs to the annual sports awards show after officially being named the host of the 2026 ESPYS.

“I started doing comedy 10 years ago, in Cleveland, Ohio, and I would take the train 12 hours to New York to sell comedy tickets on the street in Greenwich Village in exchange for stage time,” said Hernández in a press release. “It is an honor, and frankly feels crazy to be hosting the ESPYS this year in New York. I’m sure the energy is going to be great.”

The 2026 ESPYS, presented by Capital One, will take place on Wednesday, July 15, and air live at 8 p.m. on ABC. The ceremony will be held from the David H. Koch Theater at Lincoln Center in New York City.

Previous hosts include Jimmy Kimmel, John Cena, LeBron James, Serena Williams, Shane Gillis and many others. The ESPYS honor the major sports achievements of the past year. The show also celebrates unforgettable moments and recognizes notable athletes and sports figures who dominated during their seasons.

Hernández’s hosting gig comes after he performed his biggest show in Spanish during the Netflix Is A Joke festival. During the event, which was held on Mother’s Day, the comedian took a moment to honor his most loyal supporter: his mom Isabel Cancela.
“God bless all the moms out there tonight who have cared for their kids, made sacrifices,” he said in Spanish, with his mom by his side. “Today, we celebrate you.”
“God gave me a mom that worked every day for me and today I thank her and everyone who is here,” he continued.

Hernández previously shared during a January appearance on TODAY that his mom is his muse, crediting her for a lot of his material. He added that his Netflix standup, “Marcello Hernández: American Boy,” was a way for him to give her much-deserved flowers.

title: Carson Daly Chugs Beer Out Of His Shoe Live On TODAY
summary: Carson Daly tried a “shoey” live on TODAY June 3 and chugged beer out of his shoe, inspired by Ryan Reynolds and Hugh Jackman.
story: It’s not uncommon for TODAY co-hosts to try fun challenges live on air. But chugging a beer from a shoe?
TODAY co-host Carson Daly attempted his inaugural “shoey” on June 3 after watching Ryan Reynolds and Hugh Jackman partake in the beloved Australian tradition in a clip Jackman shared on Instagram.
Carson told his co-hosts he had never done a “shoey” as he started unlacing his loafers.
“Let’s go,” Jenna Bush Hager said before Carson pulled out a beer bottle from inside his suit jacket. He proceeded to pull off his shoe and pour the drink inside.
Carson chugged it down in seconds as Sheinelle Jones, Savannah Guthrie and Jenna cheered him on.
While cringing, Craig Melvin called Daly’s feat “impressive.”
Later on the plaza, Carson again brought up the “shoey.”
“Next time I’m gonna drink it out of Savannah’s shoe, and I’m gonna call it a ‘Choo-y,’” he said, referencing her Jimmy Choo heels.
Savannah offered for Carson to take on the “Choo-y” challenge right then and there, holding out her heel.
“Do you know what I have to do to stick around with these fine, hard-hitting journalists?” Carson joked. “Some interview presidents, some do ‘shoeys’ and ‘Choo-ys.’ Gotta earn your keep, everybody.”
The original clip that inspired Carson’s antics saw the “Deadpool and Wolverine” co-stars chug down their own “shoeys” with the Australian sailing team the duo recently purchased: Bonds Flying Roos.
“I would say it’s been a good 35 years since my last shoey!!!” Jackman captioned the video.

The Bonds Flying Roos will be the focus of a new Disney+ docuseries announced May 29, which will follow Reynolds, Jackman and the team through a global racing season “defined by speed, pressure and high stakes,” per a press release.
The series is the pair’s first collaboration since “Deadpool & Wolverine.”
“We once again anticipate action, comedy, heart but with a lot more water,” Jackman and Reynolds said in a statement. “And (fingers crossed) pirates.”

title: ‘Love Island USA’ Season 8 Power Rankings: The Premiere’s Winners, Losers and Surprises
summary: TODAY.com’s “Love Island USA” Season 8 commentators Tess Higgins and Sally Darr Griffin share their thoughts on the premiere.
story: In TODAY.com’s new weekly “Love Island USA” column, content creators Tess Higgins and Sally Darr Griffin break down the latest group of Season 8 episodes, sharing their thoughts on the new bombshells, couplings and dumping. Check back here over the next six weeks to read the duo’s hot takes and follow @tessdhiggins and @sallydarrgriffin on their socials to keep up with them throughout the week.
“The summer has officially begun!” host Ariana Madix announces to welcome viewers to “Love Island USA” Season 8.

The supersized premiere kicked off with an elaborate music video set to “Say You Do” by Sigala, Imani Williams and DJ Fresh that featured the first 10 islanders and Madix. Next, the cast made their way into the Fijian villa and introduced themselves to each other in the nearly two-hour episode. After the islanders shared brief facts, it was time for the first game. The islanders tested their compatibility and chemistry with each other in a game that ended with everyone making out multiple times.
Then, it was time to couple up, forming the first couples of the season. Without further ado, the couples are…
Trinity and Bryce
Trinity and Bryce
Melanie and Sincere
Melanie and Sincere
Kenzie and Zach
Kenzie and Zach
Amiya and KC
Amiya and KC
Beatriz and Sean
Beatriz and Sean
How are TODAY.com’s “Love Island USA” Season 8 commentators Tess Higgins and Sally Darr Griffin feeling about Season 8’s potential?

“This episode was an hour and 45 minutes. It was basically a movie and I did enjoy every single second of it because I love ‘Love Island.’ I’m so excited that it’s back!” Sally tells TODAY.com about the first episode. She adds that the extended premiere allowed fans to really get to know the islanders instantly.
Tess was also a fan of the episode and shares why one islander’s decision in a game could cost them a spot in the villa.
Find out what Tess and Sally thought about the initial pairs and the rest of the steamy premiere, including the two bombshells.
Best Moment

Tess: “I always love the girls getting a chance to connect alone and with Ariana (Madix) before the guys come in, because we love seeing the friendships develop on this show as the audience. It really sets the tone nicely for the group to get excited and get to know each other, like even outside of the couples.”

Sally: “I would say the bombshells coming in at the end. I was anticipating it the entire time. That’s always how a great first episode should end. It shocks the audience and it shocks the Islanders. It’s so fun to see the look on everyone’s faces. And they do it differently every time and I think the game that they played bringing them in was so fun.”
Biggest Surprise

Tess: “No one standing behind KC’s door to match with him. Maybe this was because of his, you know, ‘We all like to work out, right?’ comment that he made. Because I could see that rubbing people the wrong way, perhaps. Curious to see where things go for him.”

Sally: “I would say in the last game that they played where each person in the couple got to decide if they wanted to stay or switch. Both Trinity and Bryce decided to switch, which I thought was interesting because I thought they were having a good time. They were getting to know each other and I didn’t expect for both of them to decide to switch it up. Then also, Aniya and KC both stayed. I thought that was interesting because in the original coupling game they got together by default, and so I thought it was kind of sweet and then they decided to stay and get to know each other. I’m excited to see where they go.”
Best Couple
Tess: “So far, I’m getting a good vibe from Melanie and Sincere. They seem like a good early match to me. I think she was charmed by him, and they do have that east coast connection.”
Sally: “Melanie and Sincere. They seemed to really like each other. They’re vibing and they seem to be the strongest ones right now.”
Biggest Winner

Tess: “I thought Trinity was really funny this episode. She comes across really carefree and unafraid to speak her mind or make a bold choice. I’m also excited to see where things go for her, especially as the youngest member of the cast so far. And being paired up right now with a guy who is much older than her.”

Sally: “I would say Melanie and Sincere because they do seem to have a thing going and I’m really excited for them. I would say that they would probably be the public’s favorite at this point, so they’re the biggest winner in that sense. Then I would say also Zach because his brother is Charlie (Georgiou) from last season. All of these people are new to us, but Zach kind of has a little bit of an advantage because we feel like we know him. We know his brother and he also really looks like his brother.”
At Risk of Being Dumped

Tess: “Kenzie. You know, Zach was really giving her friend-only vibes, and there doesn’t seem to be anyone else she was connecting with right now. Seemingly she was super excited about him and into him, and it just wasn’t giving a match. Not sure where that leaves her.”

Sally: “It is early days. You never know with ‘Love Island.’ People could be dumped tomorrow. I hope not. I hope people get to stick around a little bit longer that we just got to know over the course of an hour and 45 minutes. But I would say Kenzie and Sean because they were the two people that were left alone at that final stay or switch it up game.”

title: Did the ‘Love Island USA’ Season 8 Premiere Address Vasana Montgomery’s Departure?
summary: How the Season 8 premiere of “Love Island USA” addressed Vasana Montgomery’s early departure following scandal.
story: Season 8 of “Love Island USA” began with a scandal.

Prior to the premiere on Tuesday, June 2, Peacock shared the identities of the 12 original islanders entering the villa in the first episode. But before ever seeing the cast members interact, viewers learned that one contestant had already exited the show.

Vasana Montgomery, a 25-year-old business owner from Beaverton, Oregon, was featured in the introduction video released May 28 that revealed the cast. She spoke about what she was hoping to find in the villa. But shortly after the clip was shared, fans found social media videos of Montgomery appearing to say a racial slur on camera. In one video, she seemed to say the N-word while singing a song, in another she said the same slur while playing an arcade game, according to NBC News.
On May 29, she was removed from the cast and Peacock confirmed to NBC News that she will no longer be on the reality dating show. (Peacock is owned by NBCUniversal, the parent company of TODAY.com.)
Montgomery did not respond to NBC News’ request for comment following news of her removal.

When the premiere began June 2, Montgomery was not featured at all. Instead, the episode introduced 10 islanders. The first batch of women islanders included Aniya Harvey, Beatriz Hatz, Kenzie Annis, Melanie Moreno and Trinity Tatum.
Later, two bombshells entered the villa: Gabriel Vasconcelos, who was previously revealed last week, and Kayda Bosse.

Montgomery’s exit comes after two Season 7 islanders were removed from the villa last year after they had problematic videos resurface on social media, as well. Yulissa Escobar, an original Season 7 contestant, only appeared in one episode before viewers learned in a voiceover from narrator Iain Stirling in Episode 2 that she was no longer in the villa.
Her exit came after fans discovered clips of her using racist language, including the N-word, while speaking on a podcast.
Escobar addressed the controversy on her Instagram on June 6, 2025.

“In those clips, I used a word I never should’ve used, a racial slur. I used it ignorantly, not fully understanding the weight, history, or pain behind it. I wasn’t trying to be offensive or harmful, but I recognize now that intention doesn’t excuse impact. And the impact of that word is real. It’s tied to generations of trauma, and it is not mine to use,” she wrote, in part.
She concluded, “To those who are disappointed or offended, I understand and I apologize. I am sorry.”

Fellow Season 7 contestant Cierra Ortega abruptly left toward the end of the season in Episode 30. Again, Stirling shared the news with the audience. At the time, Ortega’s old social media posts with racial slurs had gone viral.
On July 9, Ortega uploaded a nearly five-minute TikTok video apologizing for her actions.

“Before I get into the details, I want to first start by addressing not just anyone that I have hurt or deeply affected, but most importantly, the entire Asian community,” she said July 9, in part. “I am deeply, truly, honestly so sorry. I had no idea that the word held as much pain, as much harm and came with the history that it did or I never would have used it.”
She added that her post was “not an apology video” but an “accountability video.” She also said she had since educated herself about the derogatory word.

“I completely agree with the network’s decision to remove me from the villa. I think that this is something that deserved punishment, and the punishment is absolutely been received, for sure,” she said.

title: Steven Spielberg Shares the Pulled-From-the-Headlines Moment That Inspired Alien Film ‘Disclosure Day’
summary: Steven Spielberg shares what inspired his new alien movie, “Disclosure Day,” and if he believes in extraterrestrials.
story: The idea behind Steven Spielberg’s latest film, “Disclosure Day,” has a ripped-from-the-headlines origin story.

The auteur, 79, is reuniting with one of his favorite genres for his 37th movie: science fiction. “Disclosure Day,” out June 12, follows a governmental conspiracy involving the existence of extraterrestrials.

Spielberg spoke with Craig Melvin along with stars Emily Blunt, Josh O’Connor and Colman Domingo in an interview on TODAY June 3 to share why the genre has captivated him for decades and what inspired the flick’s central premise.

The filmmaker, known for hits like “Jurassic Park,” “War of the Worlds,” “Close Encounters of the Third Kind” and “ET,” said he can’t escape his fascination with whether or not humans are alone in the universe.

When Craig asked Spielberg where that curiosity originates, the Oscar winner replied, “Just a deep interest in the unknown becoming the known, you know? Ever since I was a kid, I was fascinated with the sky, and what’s up there.”
He said his attraction to science fiction is rooted in exploring the possibility of other beings visiting Earth.
“Is this something that you think that human beings are ready for?” Craig wondered.
Spielberg laughed and responded, “Well, ready or not, here it comes. I mean, that’s the premise of our film.”

He revealed he came up with the idea for “Disclosure Day” after videos surfaced a few years ago of unidentified flying objects. Following the release of the mysterious footage, Congress held its first public hearing on UFOs — now known as unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAPs) — in 50 years in May 2022. Just last month, the Pentagon shared new reports detailing decades of possible UFO sightings.
Spielberg shared that the videos and hearing in 2022 “changed everything” for him.
“It wasn’t just sensationalized,” he said. “But this was something being taken very seriously by the major news media.”
And the premise for his newest film was born.

In “Disclosure Day,” O’Connor portrays a cybersecurity expert and Blunt plays a television meteorologist. Both are trying to reveal the truth about alien life despite a government agency trying to silence them. Domingo plays a vocal advocate who is also attempting to expose the government cover-up.
While speaking with Craig, Blunt said working on the film “completely” changed her thinking about alien life.

“I felt my opinion had been changed even in prepping this movie,” she said. “I had never gone down the rabbit hole of looking at every congressional hearing that’s taken place, every documentary, docuseries, everyone who has had a genuine experience.”
“I became invested in them, in the people who had had these experiences,” she added.
As for O’Connor, he said starring in “Disclosure Day” made him develop a desire to learn more about the topic.

“I really feel a sense of, like, a hunger for this information now, because I think there aren’t many advancements or bits of knowledge that we can receive that will completely unite us than something like this. The humility that it brings, knowing that we are not alone, could be …” he shared.
“Really a positive thing,” Blunt chimed in.
“ ... In the hostile world, could unite us,” O’Connor finished.
Spielberg confirmed he is confident in his belief that there’s extraterrestrial life. But, he isn’t certain that they are living on Earth.

“I don’t have any evidence, obviously, (because) I don’t have any inside information about (aliens) living among us,” he said. “But I think the circumstantial evidence that exists right now, at this point, is overwhelmingly convincing that we are not alone.”

title: ‘Beauty and the Beast’ and ‘Whole New World’ Singer Peabo Bryson Dies After Suffering Stroke
summary: Grammy winning Peabo Bryson, known for singing “Beauty and the Beast” and “Whole New World,” died days after suffering a stroke.
story: Peabo Bryson, Grammy-winning R&B singer known for his duets “Beauty and the Beast” and “Whole New World,” has died, his family confirmed to TODAY.com.
Bryson “transitioned peacefully” on Tuesday, June 2, his family shared, adding that he was “surrounded by the love of his family and those closest to him.”

“We are tremendously moved by the outpouring of love, prayers and support from fans, friends, and colleagues around the world,” the family shared. “While our hearts are broken, we find comfort in knowing how deeply Peabo was loved and how many lives were touched by his voice and his generous spirit. His legacy and music will live on for generations to come.”
Bryson’s family did not share his cause of death.
However, a representative for the musician confirmed to TODAY.com that Bryson suffered a stroke on Thursday, May 28. Following the stroke, he remained under medical care until his passing.

Throughout his career, Bryson entertained audiences with songs like “Feel the Fire,” “I’m So Into You,” “Can You Stop the Rain,” “If Ever You’re In My Arms Again” and “Reaching for the Sky” among many others.

He earned his first Grammy in 1993 in the best pop performance by a duo or group with vocal category alongside Celine Dion for “Beauty and the Beast.” The following year, he and Regina Belle won the same award for “A Whole New World.”
The family asked for privacy during this time “as they mourn the loss of a beloved husband, father, family member, friend and artist whose impact extended far beyond the stage.”

title: When and How to Watch the ‘Love Island USA’ Season 8 Premiere
summary: When does Love Island USA start? Find out when and what time Season 8 premieres on Peacock.
story: Attention “Love Island USA” fans, the new season is about to begin!

“Love Island USA” Season 8 will soon return, with the next group of islanders ready to begin a summer filled with romance, challenges and dramatic dumpings. Leading up to the premiere, Peacock revealed the names and faces of the 12 cast members who will make up the OG islanders of the season. However shortly after, one contestant was removed after videos began circulating online of them saying a racial slur.

Now fans will watch the 11 singles enter the Fijian villa, meet for the first time and couple up during the much-anticipated season premiere. Speaking of the villa, Peacock also released a tour video led by host Ariana Madix for viewers to enjoy while they wait for the first episode to arrive.

In the clip, Madix showed off the different iconic locations around the villa, like the girls’ dressing room, photobooth and fire pit. Each space she said was “thoughtfully designed for maximum romantic impact.”
Want to make sure you don’t miss any of the steamy moments that happen in the villa this summer? Find out how to tune into “Love Island USA” Season 8, below.
When Does ‘Love Island USA’ Season 8 Start?
The newest season of “Love Island USA” will begin streaming on Tuesday, June 2.
What Time Is ‘Love Island USA’ Season 8 on?
Each episode of “Love Island USA” drops at 6 p.m. PT/ 9 p.m. ET.
Where to Watch ‘Love Island USA’ Season 8?
“Love Island USA” will stream on Peacock, which is owned by NBCUniversal, TODAY’s parent company.
Peacock will have a “Love Island USA” collection available on its homepage, with access to episodes, special clips, games and other highlights all season long.
What Days Are New Episodes of ‘Love Island USA’ Season 8 On?
New episodes of “Love Island USA” will arrive Thursdays through Tuesdays. However for the premiere week, a new episode will air on Wednesday, June 3.

The recap show, “Aftersun,” will be released on Saturdays. This season, “Summer House” star Ciara Miller and media personality Tefi Pessoa will be the new hosts of the after show, which will include never-before-seen-footage and interviews with the eliminated islanders.
There will not be an episode of “Aftersun” during premiere week on June 6. The show will resume its usual schedule, with new episodes every day except Wednesday, during the following week.

title: ‘Love Island’ Cast Season 8: Who’s Who in the Villa
summary: The official cast for “Love Island USA” Season 8 has been announced. See who will be competing and looking for love in the villa.
story: The first text of “Love Island USA” Season 8 has been sent — and it has the new islanders who are moving into the Fijian villa.

The hit Peacock reality series returns Tuesday, June 2, officially kicking off a summer of love. Fans have been eagerly awaiting the names of the islanders that they’ll watch fall in love, start drama and meet new bombshells over the next couple months. Days before the premiere, host Ariana Madix announced the 12 hopeful singles moving into the Fijian villa in a video.
“A fresh wave of islanders is moving into the villa, with a high chance of charm and flirtation,” she teased in a clip released May 28.

Then, each cast member made their cheeky introduction. Among the islanders are the daughter of a former NBA player, the brother of Season 7 British bombshell Charlie Georgiou and a Paralympian. However one contestant, Vasana Montgomery has already exited the show.
Following the casting news, viewers found social media videos of Montgomery appearing to say a racial slur multiple times. She has since been removed from the cast as of May 29.

Fans will get to see the cast meet for the first time when Season 8 premieres on Peacock Tuesday, June 2, at 6 p.m. PT/ 9 p.m. ET. New episodes will then arrive every day except Wednesday at the same time.

Returning this year is host Madix, along with narrator and comedian Iain Stirling. They’ll be joined by “Summer House” star Ciara Miller and media personality Tefi Pessoa, who are taking over as the “Aftersun” hosts. The aftershow, featuring never-before-seen footage and interviews with the dumped islanders, will air every Sunday starting June 13 at 6 p.m. PT/ 9 p.m. ET.
Plus, this season during “Aftersun” episodes Miller and Pessoa will be revealing the identities of the bombshells before they make their dramatic entrances into the villa.
Get to know the OG singles entering the villa, below.
Check back here every episode to find out who is still looking for love on Season 8 of “Love Island USA” and who has been dumped from the villa.
The Cast of ‘Love Island USA’ Season 8
Aniya Harvey
Current city: Tyrone, Georgia
Age: 23
Occupation: Marketing
Instagram: aniyaharvey02
Aniya, the daughter of a retired NBA player, says she is “a bit competitive” but still friendly. “I’m so freaking extra. Please ‘Love Island,’ give me a man!” she shouts.
Beatriz Hatz
Current city: San Diego, California
Age: 25
Occupation: Professional athlete
Instagram: bhatz_track
Beatriz was born with a “rare birth defect,” she says. But that hasn’t stopped her from becoming a Paralympian. Her type on paper? “Tall, dark and handsome.”
Bryce Dettloff
Current city: Los Angeles, California
Age: 29
Occupation: DJ
Instagram: brycealakai

Bryce says he loves music but he also works as a handyman. “I do electrical work on garbage trucks sometimes. It’s literally so disgusting but it’s like, you’ve got to do what you’ve got to do,” he says.
KC Chandler
Current city: Fresno, California
Age: 23
Occupation: Nursing assistant
Instagram: kc.thagreat
KC is a nursing assistant who says “women love men in healthcare.” He adds, “If you’re man and you have scrubs, your muscles showing a little bit, they are going for that every time.”
Kenzie Annis
Current city: Kennesaw, Georgia
Age: 24
Occupation: Nursing school graduate
Instagram: kenzieannis

Kenzie received her degree from nursing school two days before heading to the villa. “I want a huge family, five to 10 kids. I know that’s kind of crazy!” she says, adding that she would also like to adopt.
Melanie Moreno
Current city: Los Angeles, California
Age: 24
Occupation: Bikini store manager
Instagram: melanieelisa
Melanie is ready to bring her Dominican flare into the villa. Currently, she says the bikinis in the “damaged bin” of her store describe her love life. Hopefully things turn around this summer.
Sean Reifel
Current city: Easton, Pennsylvania
Age: 29
Occupation: Police officer
Instagram: parm3_sean
“You could be having the worst day of your life and I’ll just help you sift through that,” Sean says in his introduction package. He wants a woman who is thoughtful.
Sincere Rhea
Current city: Cape May, New Jersey
Age: 25
Occupation: Athlete
Instagram: sincere.rhea
Sincere is a big fan of his name and says it helps him with pickup lines. “I like to be cheesy. I often end my emails, like, ‘Sincerely Sincere.’” He also says he likes the chase.
Trinity Tatum
Current city: Newport News, Virginia
Age: 22
Occupation: Model
Instagram: soootrini
Trinity is from a small town where she says that dating a second cousin could be a possibility. “So, I just want a fresh start,” she shares. “I love a respectful man.”
Vasana Montgomery
Current city: Beaverton, Oregon
Age: 25
Occupation: Business owner
Instagram: vanity_by_vasana

After her casting was announced, videos began circulating online of her appearing to say a racial slur on camera, and she was removed from the cast by May 29. She did not respond to NBC News’ request for comment.
Peacock confirmed to NBC News that Montgomery will no longer be on the show. (Peacock is owned by NBCUniversal, the parent company of TODAY.com.)
Zach Georgiou
Current city: Birmingham, England
Age: 26
Occupation: Digital creator
Instagram: zachgeorgio

Zach is the brother of Charlie Georgiou, who was a bombshell in Season 7. Zach says he is here to see if he “can do better” during his time on the show. He has a cat named Lolo and is looking for someone who is a cat person.
Meet the ‘Love Island USA’ Season 8 Bombshells
Gabriel Vasconcelos
Current city: Miami, Florida
Age: 26
Occupation: Model
Instagram: gvasconcelosv
Gabriel wants to bring the “Brazilian fire” to Fiji. He speaks three languages: Portuguese, Spanish and English. But, he adds that making out is his love language.
Kayda Reese Bosse
Current city: Manchester, New Hampshire
Age: 22
Occupation: Business manager
Instagram: kaydabosse

Kayda, along with Gabriel, entered the villa as a bombshell in Episode 1. “I’m 10 out of 10, these men are gonna fall to their knees for me,” she said in a voiceover as she arrived. “I’m in my player mode. These other girls should definitely watch out. I’m gonna step on toes if I have to.”

title: This Celeb Has Been Tapped to Direct ‘The Seven Husbands of Evelyn Hugo’
summary: After years of trying to get “The Seven Husbands of Evelyn Hugo” movie adaptation in motion, a director has been tapped for the project.
story: It’s been a long road to get “The Seven Husbands of Evelyn Hugo” movie adaptation in motion, but it appears things may be moving forward.
Netflix announced in 2022 that it was adapting Taylor Jenkins Reid’s best-selling 2017 book into a feature film. But since then, not much has been reported about the project’s production.
However, on June 1, TODAY.com confirmed that Anna Kendrick will be directing the drama for the streaming service. Deadline was first to report the news.

This will be Kendrick’s second directorial feat. The actor previously directed and starred in the thriller “Woman of the Hour,” about a woman who goes on a dating game show and ends up on a date with a serial killer.

“The Seven Husbands of Evelyn Hugo” follows a reporter named Monique Grant who she gets the assignment of the century: interviewing the elusive Hollywood legend Evelyn Hugo, who is ready to spill her life story after years away from the spotlight.

Grant soon learns about Hugo’s early days as an aspiring actor, leading to her many, many marriages and a secret here and there. The Hollywood star is a goldmine of juicy headline-making stories, yet she is also a very complex woman who intrigues Grant.

Deadline reports that Liz Tigelaar wrote the first draft of the script and Francesca Sloane is currently working on revisions. No release date or casting news has been announced at this time but fans of the book already had someone in mind for a specific role.
The novel, a New York Times bestseller, became quite popular on BookTok with fans petitioning for Jessica Chastain to star as red-headed starlet and Hugo’s adversary Celia St. James.
However, the actor told E! News in 2023 that there was “zero possibility” she’d play Celia, adding, “I look forward to watching it and I’m sorry to disappoint everyone out there.”

Meanwhile, author Reid previously shared that she drew inspiration for the book from the late Elizabeth Taylor, who was married eight times, as well as fellow Golden Age actors Ava Gardner and Rita Hayworth.
Reid told PopSugar in 2017 that Gardner’s book “The Secret Conversations,” which was published after she died, sparked her creative process.
After reading an excerpt, the author recalled thinking to herself, “‘How great if you could do an entire book that feels like the juiciest part of this Ava Gardner book?’”

Reid continued, “So I started to think about this idea of a fictional memoir of a Hollywood star. Then I got really into the idea that you have a device of her telling her story to someone else and those two stories intersecting in interesting ways.”

title: Jennifer Lopez and Brett Goldstein Address the Rumors They’re Dating: EXCLUSIVE
summary: Jennifer Lopez and Brett Goldstein address whether they are dating in real life after sparking rumors from the set of Office Romance.
story: Jennifer Lopez and Brett Goldstein’s chemistry in their new romantic comedy is so believable that they’ve sparked dating rumors in real life.

The actors star in the new Netflix movie, “Office Romance,” which hits the streaming service on June 5. In the film, Lopez plays the CEO of an airline company who unexpectedly falls for Goldstein’s character, a lawyer who works for her company.
While appearing on TODAY June 2, the co-stars opened up about their immediate chemistry while working together.
“We were very lucky,” Goldstein said.
The actor then recalled their first encounter.
“When we first met on the Zoom, it was instant. We were laughing,” he said.
“Yes, it was easy,” Lopez agreed.
“I was like, ‘OK, it’s going to be great,” he said.

TODAY’s Savannah Guthrie then played a clip of Lopez appearing on “Watch What Happens Live With Andy Cohen.” During the episode, she was asked to name her best onscreen kiss and cited Goldstein’s smooching skills. She said the same during a prior appearance on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle.
“OK, let’s just play that again,” Goldstein joked.
“(Cohen) always corners you into saying something,” Lopez said.
“That’s why he’s good at his job. OK, well, let me see if I can channel my best Andy because you know what everyone’s saying,” Savannah said.
Lopez played coy and asked what everyone’s been talking about.
“That you might actually be dating in real life,” Savannah replied.
Lopez then took a moment to set the record straight.
“There’s never a time when I’m seen with somebody or working with somebody where they don’t try to put me with the person,” she said.
“That’s true,” Savannah replied.
“I think if you stand near her, that’s what happens,” Goldstein said.
“That’s what you wish would happen,” Savannah joked.
“Yeah, that’s why I’ve been standing so close,” he teased.
Savannah playfully pressed the issue and suggested that Lopez’s response was “not an answer.”
“That was not an answer? it was an answer!” she said.
“You didn’t say yes or no,” Savannah said.

“Because (I’m not dating) all these people that they put me (together with in rumors). I think I was with Kevin Costner this year. I was with, there was a lot of people. It happens all the time. Doesn’t make it true,” Lopez replied.
Savannah then suggested that dating rumors are inevitable when you’re a “hot, eligible single person.”
“OK, so you’re not dating? Not dating in real life,” she said.
“Not dating,” Lopez replied.
“Correct,” Goldstein said.

title: Former He-Man Star Dolph Lundgren Says Nicholas Galitzine Became the ‘Heart and Soul’ of New ‘Masters of the Universe’
summary: Nicoholas Galitzine and Dolph Lundgren talk news “Masters of the Universe” movie and how Galitzine took over the He-Man role from Lundgren.
story: It’s 1984 and you’ve just come home from school, tossed your Trapper Keeper on the counter and grabbed a few Capri Suns and Fruit Roll-Ups before settling in front of the TV. You make it through episodes of “Voltron” and “Challenge of the Go-Bots” before you reach the main event: an episode of “He-Man and the Masters of the Universe.”

For those uninitiated, the cartoon revolved around the unsure Prince Adam, who transformed into He-Man, the most powerful man in the universe, by lifting a sword over his head in front of Castle Grayskull and booming, “By the power of Grayskull! I have the power!” His wimpy pet tiger, Cringer, would then turn into the ferocious Battle Cat and together they kept the evil Skeletor from unleashing his menacing plans on the planet Eternia.

Nicholas Galitzine, who rose to fame in such non-action films as “The Idea of You” and “Red, White & Royal Blue,” tackles the role of He-Man in the latest attempt to bring him to the big screen in “Masters of the Universe,” opening in theaters June 5.
“Nick really is the heart and soul of the movie,” the film’s director, Travis Knight, tells TODAY.com in an interview ahead of its release.
“I read the script, I knew I wanted to do it. I felt like I understood the character,” Galitzine tells TODAY.com.

“He-Man” was a popular cartoon series in the 1980s and made the jump to the silver screen in 1987, with Dolph Lundgren taking the lead role in the live-action film “Masters of the Universe.” The movie flopped at the box office but has grown over the years into a cult favorite. (And here’s a fun fact to impress your friends: The movie also starred a pre-“Friends” Courteney Cox, as well as Christina Pickles, who would play her character Monica’s mother on the NBC sitcom.)

“Masters of the Universe” has appeared in various forms over the years since, but its return now is as big as He-Man’s muscles themselves, with Idris Elba, Camila Mendes, Allison Brie and Jared Leto also starring in the film.
In this version, Prince Adam winds up on Earth after he’s separated from his sword and Skeletor takes over Eternia, the beginning of Adam’s journey to take back the kingdom.
Lundgren, who has a cameo in the new movie, says Galitzine brought a new depth to He-Man.

“I thought he was great,” says Lundgren, in a joint interview with Galitzine for TODAY.com. “I was very impressed, especially with the vulnerability, and this kind of guy doesn’t believe in himself, who has been harassed by his co-workers and his boss, and it reminded me a little bit of Christopher Reeve in ‘Superman.’”

“Masters of the Universe” isn’t necessarily preachy — although fans of the original cartoon will no doubt remember the life lesson at the end of each episode — but it’s always hit the hammer on the importance of being confident and having faith in yourself. That message is front and center in this new picture, providing Galitzine the platform to not just be an action hero, but also a kind of everyman who doesn’t have it all figured out.

“When I met Nick, I knew I found my guy, because he was so funny and charming and warm,” Knight says. “He had such heart, and I knew he was the perfect guy to bring that aspect of the character to life, which for me was the most important aspect of the character.”
“He nails every single aspect of that character, which is no small feat. It’s a really challenging role, and he’s amazing at it,” he adds.
Galitzine has seen Lundgren’s film “many, many times” and watched “every piece of ‘Masters of the Universe’ property” there is in prepping for the role.
He wasn’t interested in mimicking Lundgren’s performance, though, noting Knight sought to “blend the vulnerability and the strength” of the character.

“You want to bring your own flavor. I could never do what Dolph does,” Galitzine says “He’s so special as a performer in his own way, but the way he kind of combined the sort of boyishness that he has in the movie, but also he’s so menacing and powerful when he needs to be, and I think that that is something that as He-Man is really unique.”
Lundgren, meanwhile, is over the moon that He-Man is once more front and center with a return to theaters.
“I was excited, I never thought it was going to happen, and I was excited to hear that it was a big film,” he says.

Where there’s He-Man, of course, Skeletor is not far behind. The cartoon featured Alan Oppenheimer providing the distinct voice of the character and it says something that someone so menacing  in what began as a cartoon for kids has drawn some heavy hitters for the part.

Frank Langella, who would later go on to earn an Oscar nomination for “Frost/Nixon,” portrayed the character in the 1987 movie, while Leto, himself an Oscar winner for “Dallas Buyers Club,” plays the part this time around.
Knight said Leto “loved ‘Masters of the Universe’” when he was a kid and was focused on bringing Skeletor to life.

“He had incredible ambition for our version of Skeletor,” he says. “He wanted to swing for the fences. He wanted to come up with an iconic villain on screen. His version of Skeletor, or our version of Skeletor, was something special and something memorable, and I loved that ambition.”
“He’s an over-the-top villain, he’s funny, he’s awful, but above all else, he’s incredibly entertaining, and I think I could not be more proud of the version of Skeletor that we created,” he adds.

Cinephiles may also notice a tip of the cap to another blast from the past with the music. The score, done by Daniel Pemberton, features guitar work from Queen legend Brian May. It’s a sound similar to the music Queen famously made for the 1980s film “Flash Gordon.”
That was “by design,” says Knight.

“As soon as you hear those sounds come out of the speaker of that, that Brian May guitar sound, which is unlike anything else, it was magic, like the hair on the back of your neck would stand up,” he says.

With its appreciation for the He-Man of yore, along with a sound and look that screams “popcorn flick,” “Masters of the Universe” is a mix of action and adventure, with a dollop of humor that keeps things from getting too heavy. It looks back while also plowing straight ahead.
“It’s so epic, it’s so much fun,” Galitzine says.
“It’s about finding your inner power, it’s about believing in yourself, and it’s done in a very charming way,” Lundgren says.

“It’s a blast. It’s funny. It’s engaging. It’s got great action. It’s got big heart, and it makes you feel things. It has something to say, so it’s got a lot of different elements at play,” Knight says.

title: The Message Dua Lipa’s Wedding Look Is Sending, According to 3 Fashion Experts
summary: What is the meaning of Dua Lipa’s wedding dress and outfit? Details are explained by the Knot’s editorial director, a fashion expert and a stylist.
story: Dua Lipa made a fashion statement with her regal wedding look.
The Grammy-winning singer, 30, married actor Callum Turner, 36, on May 31 in a ceremony held at Old Marylebone Town Hall in London, The Associated Press confirmed.

Shortly after their nuptials, the couple was photographed exiting the hall hand in hand, with loved ones surrounding them. While Turner opted for a classic navy suit, Lipa stunned in a Schiaparelli skirt suit, resembling an old Hollywood star. She paired the look with a wide-brimmed Stephen Jones white hat and matching gloves fit for a queen.
